title: SSH
desc: List LAN devices and configure username/port per target, copy the connect command in one click. Configs sync across devices.
emptyHint: Discovered LAN devices appear above automatically, or add an IP manually.
guideRemoteTitle: Enable SSH on the target device
guideLocalMac: ssh is built in: run ssh user@ip in Terminal. Use ssh-keygen for keys and ~/.ssh/config aliases for passwordless login.
guideLocalUbuntu: The client is usually installed; just run ssh user@ip. If not: sudo apt install openssh-client.
guideLocalWindows: Windows 10+ ships OpenSSH client; run ssh user@ip in PowerShell.
guideRemoteMac: System Settings → General → Sharing → enable Remote Login; ensure your user is in the allowed list.
guideRemoteUbuntu: Install and start the service: sudo apt install openssh-server && sudo systemctl enable --now ssh. If ufw is enabled: sudo ufw allow ssh.
guideRemoteWindows: Settings → Apps → Optional features → install OpenSSH Server; in admin PowerShell run Start-Service sshd and Set-Service -Name sshd -StartupType Automatic, then allow port 22 in the firewall.
